title: Dockerfile Frontend, Build Context, Instructions, and Process Contracts · Container Repair Bay
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective reviewed application Dockerfile and build-context contract; isolate the first causal fault in dockerfile frontend, build context, instructions, and process contracts and preserve a regression check before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in reviewed application Dockerfile and build-context contract and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory…
objectives: Use a pinned stable Dockerfile syntax frontend and explain parser directives, escape behavior, comments, continuations, and version boundaries.
Define local, Git, named, empty, and remote build contexts and use Dockerfile-specific ignore rules to minimize authority and invalidation.
Use FROM, WORKDIR, COPY, and justified ADD behavior with explicit ownership, paths, checksums, and context boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Dockerfile Syntax Parser Directives
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug build sends a 780 MiB context containing .git and local.env, uses a floating base, copies the repository, runs as root, and wraps the process in /bin/sh. Redesign context and runtime contracts. Competency probe: Use a pinned stable Dockerfile syntax frontend and explain parser directives, escape behavior, comments, continuations, and version boundaries.

Inspect the Dockerfile Syntax Parser Directives trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Dockerfile Syntax Parser Directives.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use a pinned stable dockerfile syntax frontend and explain parser directives, escape behavior, comments, continuations, and version boundaries. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Dockerfile Syntax Parser Directives, keep Dockerfile Syntax Parser Directives connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

Redesign context and runtime contracts. Competency probe: Use a pinned stable Dockerfile syntax frontend and explain parser directives, escape behavior, comments, continuations, and version boundaries.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Dockerfile Cmd Entrypoint Contract
steps.instruction:
Append the Docker evidence item headed "  # Evidence: dockerfile-cmd-entrypoint-contract-1cxzcx". Tailor its Dockerfile change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run Docker commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Dockerfile Cmd Entrypoint Contract.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Dockerfile Cmd Entrypoint Contract into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Dockerfile Cmd Entrypoint Contract, keep Dockerfile Cmd Entrypoint Contract connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: dockerfile-cmd-entrypoint-contract-1cxzcx
  - competency: dockerfile-cmd-entrypoint-contract
    artifact: Dockerfile
    decision: dockerfile cmd entrypoint contract
    scenario-variant: 1
    environment: native amd64 engine with a cold content store
    fault-injection: fill the writable layer before graceful termination
    retained-proof: attached SBOM provenance signature and policy decision
    case-key: 1cxzcx
    change: |
      FROM docker.io/library/alpine:3.22@sha256:verified AS runtime
      WORKDIR /app
      COPY --link public/ ./public/
      USER 10001:10001
      ENTRYPOINT ["/app/status"]
    verify: docker buildx build --check --progress=plain .
    failure: Reject a floating base, secret-bearing context, shell-form signal wrapper, root runtime, or copied repository wildcard.
    repair: Pin the reviewed base digest, shrink .dockerignore context, use explicit COPY, set numeric non-root identity, and test argv behavior.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Dockerfile Cmd Entrypoint Contract, name the daemon authority, immutable identity, process, storage, network, secret, and cleanup boundary, Dockerfile Cmd Entrypoint Contract precondition, or invariant that must hold.

Redesign context and runtime contracts. Competency probe: Compose ENTRYPOINT and CMD into an override-friendly PID 1 contract and test arguments, signals, and exit status.

For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Dockerfile Cmd Entrypoint Contract, challenge the documented misconception with manifest, inspect, lifecycle, failure, repair, and transfer evidence: Multiple CMD instructions combine into a sequence of default commands.
